title: Tapinanthus nigritanus
taxonomy: Tapinanthus nigritanus is a species of mistletoe in the family Loranthaceae. It is a parasitic plant that grows on the branches of its host tree, typically in the tropical regions of sub-Saharan Africa. The Loranthaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, with over 70 genera and more than 1,000 species. This family is commonly known as the mistletoe family and is found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. The classification of Tapinanthus nigritanus is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Magnoliophyta, and the class Magnoliopsida.
